title: Crepidomanes australiense
taxonomy: Crepidomanes australiense is a species of fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae. It belongs to the genus Crepidomanes, which is a group of small to medium-sized ferns. Crepidomanes australiense is further classified as a member of the subfamily Trichomattoideae and the order Hymenophytaceae. The species is native to Australia and is often found in tropical and subtropical regions. Its classification is consistent with the APG IV plant classification system.
uses: Crepidomanes australiense, also known as the Australian climbing fern, has been used in traditional medicine in Australia for various purposes. The plant has been employed to treat ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. Its leaves and stems are said to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. However, further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness of Crepidomanes australiense in treating these conditions. Traditional healers in Australia continue to use this plant, but it is not widely recognized by modern medicine.
